settings.label: Tall fescue mowing default
settings.notes: Avoid scalping and avoid removing more than one-third of the blade.

settings.label: Scotts Elite spreader default
settings.value: Follow product label; record actual setting after use
settings.notes: Future Codex updates should add exact product/model settings here when known.

settings.label: Hose-end liquid application default
settings.value: Use Ortho Dial N Spray for hose-end / liquid application scenarios
settings.notes: Follow product-specific label rates and keep incompatible products separate.

settings.label: Pump Sprayer #1
settings.value: Reserved for Roundup Weed & Grass Killer
settings.notes: Non-selective weed killer sprayer. Keep away from grass-safe products.

settings.label: Full lawn coverage math
settings.value: 8,225 sq ft total lawn; one 5,000 sq ft product covers about 61%; two units usually cover full-property planning
settings.notes: Use this for planning granular product purchases without turning the app into live stock tracking.

settings.label: Active irrigation runtime
settings.value: 84 minutes per watering day across active zones
settings.notes: Active watering days are Sunday, Tuesday, and Thursday at 6:00 AM start time.

settings.label: Pre-emergent water-in
settings.notes: Avoid heavy storm washout immediately after application.

settings.label: Mulch cycle
settings.notes: Based on the large 2025 mulch purchase; adjust after bed condition review.

lessonsLearned: Healthy tall fescue around Valley Center, KS is mostly about timing, restraint, and fall investment.
Avoid heavy early spring fertilizing unless summer irrigation and mowing follow-through are realistic.
Fall seeding and fall fertilizer usually matter more than chasing July color.
Weather windows matter for product applications: wind, rain, heat, and freeze risk can change the answer.
Products are tracked as usual buys and typical use references, not as exact live stock counts.
